- Goal model: Move the green cross objects to fill the gap position.
- Recent findings: Current state has no G frame objects. All 5 green crosses remain unchanged. The clicks may have been just selecting without any lasting effect.
- Open questions: What's the actual goal? Need to try combining selection with other actions (directional keys or ACTION7).
- Plan: Select the top N object and move it UP into the gap, then move the bottom N up.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model.

You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.

When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.

sections.content:
The click on the N object didn't seem to change anything visible. The board still looks the same. Let me look at the transition to see if anything changed.
Looking at the images:
- Step 20: G frame on top N
- Step 21: G frame on top N (same)
So clicking on the N object didn't change the G frame position or anything else visible.
Let me reconsider the problem. The G frame seems to be a selection indicator. Directional keys move the G frame between N objects. But what actually moves the N objects themselves?
Wait - maybe I need to think about this differently. The G frame is currently on the top N. What if I need to move the N object by clicking on an empty space where I want it to go? Or what if I need to use a different action?
Let me look at the gap area more carefully. The gap is at rows 24-27, cols 41-44. It's a hollow square (gray border with white inside). Maybe I need to click inside the gap to "place" the selected N object there?
Let me try clicking inside the gap area, around row 25-26, col 42-43.

<function=python>
<parameter=code>

print("Last action result:", last_action_result)
print("Board changed:", last_action_result.get('board_changed'))

# The gap is at rows 24-27, cols 41-44
# Let me try clicking inside the gap to place the selected N there
# The center of the gap would be around row 25-26, col 42-43

for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'w':  # white - might be gap interior
        print(f"w node: pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary'][:3]}...")

# Try clicking in the gap center
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 25, 'col': 42}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
